text:
Good evening, friends. Happy to be here again tonight, to greet you in the name of the Lord. I just got in on time, didn't I? I want to be a little quicker than that when I get to heaven; be just a little bit more on time. Well, we had to kind of hurry; had quite a time parking.
Is everybody feeling good? That's good. That's very fine. Now, today has been a great day for me. I just have… Seemed like today, the Lord's blessings just seem to be pouring in. And if I could just get enough time to get to all the places are calling to come is the only thing. I think there's about four hundred, or better, lays constantly, of the places of the cities.
And the poor people are needy, and I pray that God will just be good enough to me, to inspire me to let me know which place to go to … just what to do.
text_no:
God kveld, venner. Det er en glede å være her igjen i kveld og hilse dere i Herrens navn. Jeg kom akkurat i tide, gjorde jeg ikke? Jeg vil være litt raskere når jeg kommer til himmelen; være akkurat passe på tid. Vel, vi måtte skynde oss litt; det var utfordrende å finne parkering.
Føler alle seg bra? Det er godt å høre. Det har vært en flott dag for meg i dag. Det virker som Herrens velsignelser bare har strømmet på i dag. Den eneste utfordringen er å finne nok tid til å besøke alle stedene som ber meg komme. Jeg tror det hele tiden er rundt fire hundre steder, eller flere, i byene som venter.
De trengende menneskene trenger hjelp, og jeg ber om at Gud vil være god nok mot meg til å inspirere meg til å vite hvilke steder jeg skal dra til ... og hva jeg skal gjøre.

text:
And I have something on my heart tonight that I… This morning, Billy Paul come up and was telling me about the meeting last evening. And he gave me a letter from Sister Caubles. And it was about a little child that I had prayed for up in Chicago. And it's just been on my heart all day long to find the predicament the mother and father had…
Maybe some night I'd like to have her, or someone to read that letter to the audience: just a typical letter of people in need. But, the poor little baby, I didn't know it… 'Course, it just come through the line. And the mother said the Holy Spirit just began to reveal, and tell everything right up to the case, come on up, till just at the present time and then stop. Didn't know what to say from then on.
And she's frantically… The little baby, I believe, said had cancer, and had part of his tongue taken out and his throat in here. And it's… One of the pastors at the Moody Bible in Chicago; he's a missionary, just returned in from Africa; I think that's French Africa … French West Africa. And he got a letter a few days ago, both of them, that their little church is about gone; and Communism is sweeping over, taking it. And, oh, just think of the predicament.
Now their little baby laying there just at the point of death, looking for it to die at any time. And, there the little church swept away; Communism is taking things over. I just couldn't keep from crying a little. I said, "God, if You'll just… I don't know what You're going to do. But, if You'll just spare that baby's life, I'll promise You that'll be a open door for me to West Africa where the Communism is taking over the churches over there … taking over their church."
And I want you to pray with me that God will do something and help. Of course, we want His will done in every case.
text_no:
Jeg har noe på hjertet i kveld som jeg… I morges kom Billy Paul og fortalte meg om møtet i går kveld. Han ga meg et brev fra Søster Caubles. Det handlet om et lite barn jeg hadde bedt for i Chicago. Det har opptatt meg hele dagen å tenke på den vanskelige situasjonen foreldrene befinner seg i.
Kanskje jeg en kveld ønsker at hun, eller noen andre, kan lese det brevet for publikum: det er bare et typisk brev fra folk i nød. Men den stakkars lille babyen… Jeg visste ikke det. Selvfølgelig kom det bare gjennom bønnekøen. Moren sa at Den Hellige Ånd begynte å åpenbare og fortelle alt opp til saken, helt fram til den nåværende tid, for så å stoppe. Visste ikke hva jeg skulle si derfra.
Og hun er desperat… Den lille babyen, tror jeg, hadde kreft, og deler av tungen og halsen var fjernet. En av pastorene ved Moody Bible Institute i Chicago, som nettopp hadde returnert fra fransk Vest-Afrika, hadde fått et brev for noen dager siden, både han og kona. Deres lille menighet er nesten borte; kommunismen tar over. Tenk på den situasjonen.
Nå ligger deres lille baby på dødens rand, forventet å dø når som helst. Og deres lille menighet er i ferd med å bli utslettet av kommunismen. Jeg klarte ikke å holde tårene tilbake. Jeg sa: "Gud, hvis Du bare… Jeg vet ikke hva Du vil gjøre. Men, hvis Du bare redder den babyens liv, lover jeg Deg at det vil bli en åpen dør for meg til Vest-Afrika hvor kommunismen tar over menighetene der… tar over deres menighet."
Jeg ønsker at dere skal be med meg om at Gud vil gjøre noe og hjelpe. Selvfølgelig vil vi at Hans vilje skal skje i hvert tilfelle.

text:
I believe I was testifying the other night about George Wright. I just happened to see him sitting here. I have to… Here was a man that they'd give up to die, and I seen a vision the same time Mrs. Baker's daughter… Here he is, here that he would never be up any more and he's crippled; his legs had pulled him up like this. And here he is tonight by the power of God. The same night the vision come from Mrs. Baker, her daughter…
Are you here, Mrs. Baker? Is the girl here, too? Is your daughter? [Mrs. Baker testifies about her daughter.] Praise the Lord. It's wonderful; it certainly is. Well, it's the same time that her daughter, while I was in the hospital, that they's calling that they didn't think that the young lady was going to live. And when I looked at her, I didn't think she was either. And so we walked in, and while standing, praying, I saw that light come over her bed. That's when it told her what would happen just exactly the way, and the time, she'd be sent home; and she was.
text_no:
Forleden kveld vitnet jeg om George Wright. Jeg så ham tilfeldigvis sitte her. Her er en mann som de sa kom til å dø, men jeg så en visjon samtidig med at Mrs. Bakers datter... Her er han nå, etter at de sa at han aldri ville stå opp igjen, og han var krøplet; bena hadde trukket seg sammen på denne måten. Og her er han i kveld takket være Guds kraft. Den samme natten kom visjonen også om Mrs. Baker og datteren hennes...
Er du her, Mrs. Baker? Er datteren din her også? [Mrs. Baker vitner om datteren.] Pris Herren. Det er vidunderlig; det er det virkelig. Vel, samtidig som visjonen om datteren hennes kom da jeg var på sykehuset, trodde ikke folk at den unge damen kom til å overleve. Da jeg så på henne, trodde ikke jeg det heller. Så vi gikk inn, og mens vi stod og ba, så jeg lyset komme over sengen hennes. Det fortalte hva som ville skje og nøyaktig når hun ville bli sendt hjem; og det skjedde.

text:
And the very same night, I'd been up to Lexington, Kentucky, come back down, and got home about daylight. And got in bed and slept till about nine o'clock; and got up and started out through the hall. And as I did, there was a young lady standing there and she was speaking. And I thought, Well, what's she doing standing here?
And she said, "Mother, that's what Brother Branham said."
I looked over and seen her mother talking on the telephone at her home. And I knew it was a vision then. Then I heard something like clods dropping. And I just left Brother Wright's two days before that where I told his son, and all of them, that perhaps he was going to die. I'd been down four or five days praying for him … that he was going to die. The doctors done give him up and everything.
And then, I heard them clods dropping. There'd been some people there who doesn't believe in divine healing. The church that he used to belong to---not any criticism---the Church Of Christ, and they don't believe in it.
So, they'd been up there making fun of him because he had me down there praying for him after the doctor said he was going to die. And when I looked, there was clods dropping under a willow tree. I never knew the man dug graves. And he said, "Thus saith the Lord: He'll dig the grave for some of them that's laughing at him." Here he is.
text_no:
Samme natt hadde jeg vært oppe i Lexington, Kentucky, og kommet tilbake hjem ved daggry. Jeg la meg og sov til rundt klokken ni. Jeg stod opp og gikk ut i gangen. Der sto en ung dame som snakket, og jeg tenkte: Hva gjør hun her?
Hun sa: "Mor, det var det Bror Branham sa."
Jeg så og oppdaget at moren hennes snakket i telefonen hjemme. Da visste jeg at det var et syn. Så hørte jeg noe som hørtes ut som jordklumper som falt. To dager tidligere hadde jeg vært hos Bror Wright og fortalt hans sønn og de andre at han kanskje skulle dø. Jeg hadde vært der i fire eller fem dager og bedt for ham, da doktorene hadde gitt ham opp.
Jeg hørte jordklumper falle. Det hadde vært noen der som ikke trodde på guddommelig helbredelse. Menigheten han tidligere tilhørte---ikke for å kritisere---var Kristi Menighet, og de tror ikke på det.
De hadde vært der og gjort narr av ham fordi jeg hadde vært der og bedt for ham etter at legene sa han skulle dø. Da jeg så, falt jordklumper under et piletre. Jeg visste ikke at mannen gravde graver. Og han sa: "Så sier Herren: Han skal grave graven til noen av dem som ler av ham." Her er han.

text:
I called Mrs. Wright on the phone, and told her right away what had happened. And I said, "When I come into the building one of these days, he'll be walking right through the tabernacle, make a left turn, and come over and shake my hand, and say, "Brother Bill…"
And then, Sunday, first time I'd been around for quite awhile. I didn't even know the man, where he was at; walked right out of the tabernacle without him knowing this, walked right the same way. And I just stopped and waited and watched. Come right around the same way, the way it was said, walked toward me, and took a hold of my hand: just exactly the way it said.
Now, when I say anything, it could be wrong; but when you hear Him say something, it's just that way. He's the Lord. Aren't we so happy for those things?
text_no:
Jeg ringte til fru Wright og fortalte henne umiddelbart hva som hadde skjedd. Jeg sa: "En dag når jeg kommer inn i bygningen, vil han gå rett gjennom tabernaklet, ta til venstre og komme bort for å håndhilse på meg og si: 'Bror Bill…'"
På søndag, for første gang på lenge, var jeg tilstede igjen. Jeg kjente ikke engang mannen eller visste hvor han var, men han gikk ut av tabernaklet uten å vite dette. Han gikk akkurat på samme måte som forutsagt. Jeg stoppet opp, ventet og så på. Han kom akkurat på samme måte, gikk mot meg, tok tak i hånden min på nøyaktig samme måte som det var sagt.
Når jeg sier noe, kan det være feil; men når du hører Ham si noe, er det akkurat slik. Han er Herren. Er vi ikke så lykkelige for disse tingene?

text:
I was just thinking, today, of what our Lord has did for us. My wife … I caught her crying today. And, I come out of studying, and she was crying. I said, "What's the matter?"---just a little something. This is my home country, you know; it's hard here, hardest place in the world.
And, you can notice a difference. It was something about school children, and you know how women are: They're so tedious about those things. And some people that goes to a little better church than what our tabernacle'd be over there. People's got nothing against me, now. They like me. And they like… But, they'd rather their children wouldn't associate with our children as long they could be with the children that goes to their own church, or something on that order, you know. And, it kind of hurt the wife a little bit. And she said, "Well, I keep my children clean, and we try…"
I said, "That's not it, honey." I said, "That's not the idea. Them pastors, they talk, say… Here the other day, a minister friend of mine, standing … didn't know that he was sitting right in the congregation with these fellows, and my name came up.
He said, "He's a fine boy. We all love him and like him. But, he's kind of an illiterate type, and he doesn't speak well."
That's truth, you know. That's really true. But, I just happened to think that's the same words they said about our Lord---same thing. These groups that He traveled with: the common people…
The Bible said, "The common people heard Him gladly." Just the common and the illiterate, and so forth. And He never had any doctor's degree, or whatever, as far as I know. They didn't even record He ever went to school, but He was the Son of God just the same. That's the main thing.
text_no:
I dag tenkte jeg på hva vår Herre har gjort for oss. Min kone—jeg fant henne gråtende i dag. Jeg kom ut fra studiet, og hun gråt. Jeg spurte: "Hva er i veien?"—bare noe lite. Dette er hjemstedet mitt, vet du; det er hardt her, det hardeste stedet i verden.
Og du kan merke en forskjell. Det handlet om skolebarn, og du vet hvordan kvinner er: De er så nøye med slike ting. Og noen mennesker som går til en litt bedre menighet enn vår tabernakel over der. Folk har ikke noe imot meg, egentlig. De liker meg. Men, de ville heller at deres barn ikke skulle omgås våre barn så lenge de kunne være med barna som går til deres egen menighet, eller noe i den retning. Og det såret kona litt. Og hun sa: "Vel, jeg holder barna mine rene, og vi prøver..."
Jeg sa: "Det er ikke det, kjære." Jeg sa: "Det er ikke poenget. De pastorene snakker, de sier... Her om dagen, en predikantvenn av meg, stående... visste ikke at han satt rett i menigheten blant disse mennene, og navnet mitt kom opp."
Han sa: "Han er en fin gutt. Vi alle elsker ham og liker ham. Men, han er en slags analfabet, og han snakker ikke godt."
Det er sant, vet du. Det er virkelig sant. Men, jeg kom akkurat til å tenke på at det er de samme ordene de sa om vår Herre—samme ting. Disse gruppene som Han reiste med: vanlige folk...
Bibelen sier, "De vanlige folk hørte Ham gjerne." Bare vanlige og analfabeter, og så videre. Og Han hadde aldri noen doktorgrad eller hva det måtte være, så vidt jeg vet. De registrerte ikke engang at Han noensinne gikk på skolen, men Han var Guds Sønn likevel. Det er det viktigste.

text:
So, now, we want to talk just a few moments. Awhile ago, there was such a terrible emergency. We haven't been taking calls during the time of the meetings, because you just get so… But, there was a lady who called, was in a desperate condition. And my wife come into the room, said, "Honey, I hate to call you from study, but," said, "this is just such an awful emergency." And said, "The woman's fixing to commit suicide."
And, so I got to the phone. And while standing at the phone, the Holy Spirit told her just who she was, what she was doing, how she was dressed, what was the matter with her and what caused it; and healed her right there while we was standing at the phone.
Oh, my. I tell you. I heard crying, her mother screaming, holding her and everything, till I… And her just praising the Lord, she… So, He's good all around. If you just think of how… Compare that with the New Testament early church. Just compare it and see how it coincides with the New Testament church, the apostles, how the visions came, how everything… How the Lord worked.
text_no:
La oss nå snakke et øyeblikk. For litt siden var det en veldig alvorlig nødssituasjon. Vi har ikke tatt imot samtaler under møtene, fordi det blir for mye... Men en kvinne ringte i en desperat tilstand. Min kone kom inn i rommet og sa: "Honey, jeg hater å forstyrre deg i studiet, men dette er en forferdelig nødsituasjon." Hun sa: "Kvinnen er i ferd med å begå selvmord."
Så jeg tok telefonen. Mens jeg snakket med henne, fortalte Den Hellige Ånd henne nøyaktig hvem hun var, hva hun gjorde, hvordan hun var kledd, hva problemet var og hva som forårsaket det. Hun ble helbredet der og da mens vi sto og snakket i telefonen.
Å, min. Jeg kan fortelle deg. Jeg hørte henne gråte, moren hennes skrike og holde henne, og alt mulig, til jeg... Og hun bare priste Herren. Han er virkelig god hele veien. Tenk på hvordan... Sammenlign det med den tidlige kirken i Det Nye Testamentet. Bare sammenlign det og se hvordan det sammenfaller med den tidlige kirken i Det Nye Testamentet, apostlene, hvordan visjonene kom, hvordan alt... Hvordan Herren virket.

text:
Here I was going over to Africa; supposed to be in India this month. Had a date sent and everything. Done sent and had time set with dinner with Mr. Nehru, before going, and the Prime Minister. That was William Branham doing that. And I was all fixed and getting my visa ready.
I was praying for some people in the house, and went out in my house, went into another room. Come back through to answer the telephone, and come through the little hall, going back into another room (There'd been a couple more carloads come in since I was in the room), answering this phone: emergency long-distance.
And when I started through the room, there stood a man standing there. He had a towel, looked like, over his head like this, hanging down. He was kind of a dark-complected looking fellow. Looked like his nose was kind of pushed on the end, or like a kind of stump like that. And he was standing there looking right at me. I thought he'd been a patient just come in. And I said, "How do you do, sir." And he was standing right in my road. So I said, "How do you do, sir?"
And he said … looked up like that, and said, "Brother Branham, don't go overseas till September."
Now, I said, "What…?"
Well, I looked and the man was gone. Now, if that ain't just like the Macedonian call that went over to [unclear words]. Just the same Angel of the Lord, see.
text_no:
Jeg var på vei til Afrika; jeg skulle egentlig være i India denne måneden. Jeg hadde fått alle detaljene, inkludert en middag med Mr. Nehru og statsministeren. Alt var klart, og visaen min var i orden.
Jeg ba for noen mennesker i huset, gikk inn i et annet rom og måtte svare på telefonen. Da jeg gikk gjennom gangen på vei til et annet rom (det hadde kommet noen flere bilfulle med folk siden jeg var sist der), måtte jeg svare på en nødtelefon.
Når jeg gikk gjennom rommet, sto en mann der. Han hadde et håndkle over hodet som hang ned. Han var mørkhudet og så ut som om nesen hans var litt trykket på enden. Han sto der og så rett på meg. Jeg trodde han var en pasient som nettopp hadde kommet. Jeg sa: "God dag, sir." Han sto rett i veien for meg.
Han så opp og sa: "Bror Branham, ikke dra utenlands før september."
Jeg sa: "Hva...?"
Da jeg så opp, var mannen borte. Det var som det makedonske ropet i Apostlenes gjerninger. Det var den samme Herrens Engel.

text:
What is it? It's the Lord Jesus that loves us all. He loves all you. He's right here tonight. He might not… He might deal with you in another way, but it's the same Lord. And He might deal with you in a loving heart. That's the same Lord. He might give you a passion for lost souls. It's the same Lord, see.
But He set some in the church, teachers, apostles, prophets, gifts of healing, working of miracles; and all those things are set in the church. Do you believe that? I never did see where they'd be set out of the church. He set them in the church for the perfecting of the church and to be in the church until He comes again.
Now, in those things… Now, many times, of course, I know, Christian friends, we have a lot of impersonations on those things. That's to be expected; that's always. We said the other night in one of our lessons, when Israel went up, a mixed multitude went with them. That's true.
But now, what you are, you are by the grace of God. "What God hath…" You're a Christian, not because you sought God; but because God sought you. That's right, see. Man can't seek God---he just can't; it's his nature to be a sinner. Just like the pig: Tell him he's wrong eating slop; you couldn't do it. He's a pig to begin with. So you couldn't change that. He's just to begin… But God seeks after man. And man comes to God; and when he comes to God, He gives him everlasting life.
text_no:
Hva er det? Det er Herren Jesus som elsker oss alle. Han elsker dere alle. Han er her i kveld. Han kan behandle deg på en annen måte, men det er samme Herre. Han kan behandle deg med et kjærlig hjerte. Det er samme Herre. Han kan gi deg en lidenskap for tapte sjeler. Det er samme Herre.
Men Han har satt noen i menigheten: lærere, apostler, profeter, gaver til helbredelse, miraklenes gjerninger; og alt dette er satt i menigheten. Tror du det? Jeg har aldri sett at de skulle være utenfor menigheten. Han satte dem i menigheten for å fullkommengjøre den, og for at de skal være der til Han kommer igjen.
Nå, mange ganger, selvfølgelig, vet jeg, kristne venner, vi har mange etterligninger av disse tingene. Det må forventes; det har alltid vært slik. Vi sa forleden natt i en av våre leksjoner at da Israel dro opp, gikk en blandet mengde med dem. Det er sant.
Men det du er, er du ved Guds nåde. "Hva Gud har…" Du er en kristen, ikke fordi du søkte Gud, men fordi Gud søkte deg. Det er riktig. Mennesket kan ikke søke Gud---han bare kan ikke; hans natur er å være en synder. Akkurat som grisen: Fortell ham at han tar feil i å spise avfall; du kunne ikke gjøre det. Han er en gris til å begynne med. Så du kunne ikke forandre det. Men Gud søker etter mennesket. Og når mennesket kommer til Gud, gir Han ham evig liv.

text:
We want to read some of the Word just in a moment. Now… And tonight, if possible, I just want to speak, just a few moments, and maybe call up some of those prayer cards that we left off last week … or, last Sunday. We've been having more or less meetings of preaching this week, because we've been waiting for an audience. This is a fleece before the Lord.
Now, let's bow our heads just a moment to talk to the Author before we read His Word.
Our kind, loving, Heavenly Father, we approach Thee, tonight, in the lovely name of Thy Son, Jesus Christ. We love Him because He first loved us and gave His life for us. And we who were once alienated from God, cut off without mercy, without hope in the world; sinners by nature, dying in disgrace, going to a devil's hell in endless eternity. While we were in this condition---enemies of God---God sent His own Son, made in the likeness of sinful flesh; took upon Himself the form of man; suffered, bled, and died to redeem us back to the Father. And now we are sons and daughters of God by His grace. Oh, how we thank Thee for it.
Great One, in Your majesty, tonight, we pray that You'll bless our coming together. We're not worthy to have these blessings, but we ask them because Thou has promised them. And what You promise, that You will do, O God. In these years of service, we have found You to be just exactly what You promised.
And we pray tonight, now, that the Holy Spirit will be here---the resurrected Lord Jesus---and will spread forth His great power over this audience. Save the lost, recall the backsliders back home, heal the sick, rejoice with Your saints, for we ask it in His name. Amen.
text_no:
Vi skal lese litt fra Ordet om et øyeblikk. I kveld ønsker jeg, om mulig, å tale noen få minutter og kanskje kalle opp noen av dem med bønnekortene vi ikke rakk sist uke, eller forrige søndag. Denne uken har vi hatt flere møter med forkynnelse fordi vi har ventet på en større forsamling. Dette er et tegn for Herren.
La oss nå bøye våre hoder et øyeblikk og henvende oss til Forfatteren før vi leser Hans Ord.
Vår gode, kjærlige, Himmelske Far, vi nærmer oss Deg i kveld i Din Sønns, Jesus Kristus, herlige navn. Vi elsker Ham fordi Han elsket oss først og ga sitt liv for oss. Vi som en gang var fremmede for Gud, avskåret uten nåde, uten håp i verden; syndere av naturen, døende i skam, på vei til en djevels helvete i en endeløs evighet. Mens vi var i denne tilstanden—Guds fiender—sendte Gud Sin egen Sønn, gjort i syndig kjødets liknelse; tok på Seg menneskets form; led, blødde og døde for å kjøpe oss tilbake til Faderen. Og nå er vi Guds sønner og døtre ved Hans nåde. Å, hvor vi takker Deg for det.
Store En, i Din majestet, ber vi i kveld om at Du vil velsigne vårt samvær. Vi er ikke verdige til å motta disse velsignelsene, men vi ber om dem fordi Du har lovet dem. Og hva Du lover, det vil Du oppfylle, O Gud. Gjennom disse årene med tjeneste har vi funnet Deg å være akkurat som Du har lovet.
Vi ber nå i kveld om at Den Hellige Ånd vil være her—den oppstandne Herren Jesus—og vil spre Sin store kraft over denne forsamlingen. Redd de fortapte, kall de frafalne tilbake hjem, helbred de syke, gled Deg med Dine hellige, for vi ber om dette i Hans navn. Amen.

text:
In Saint Luke, the eighth chapter, and beginning with the fiftieth verse we read this:
But when Jesus heard it, he answered him, saying, Fear not: only believe, and she shall be made whole.
And when he came into the house, he suffered no man to go in but Peter, and James, and John, and the father and the mother of the maiden.
And all wept, and bewailed her: but he said, Weep not; she is not dead, but sleepeth.
And they laughed him to scorn, knowing that she was dead.
And he put them all out, and took her by the hand, and called, saying, Maid, arise.
And the spirit came again, and she arose straightway: and he commanded to give her meat.
And her parents were astonished: but he charged them that she … tell no man what was done.
text_no:
I Evangeliet etter Lukas, det åttende kapittel, begynner vi med det femtiende verset og leser dette:
Men da Jesus hørte det, svarte Han og sa: Frykt ikke, bare tro, og hun skal bli helbredet.
Da Han kom inn i huset, tillot Han ingen å gå inn bortsett fra Peter, Jakob, Johannes og jentas far og mor.
Alle gråt og klaget over henne, men Han sa: Gråt ikke; hun er ikke død, hun sover.
De lo Han til spott, fordi de visste at hun var død.
Så drev Han alle ut, tok henne i hånden og sa: Jente, stå opp.
Hennes ånd vendte tilbake, og hun reiste seg straks. Og Han befalte at hun skulle få noe å spise.
Foreldrene hennes var forbløffet, men Han påla dem å ikke fortelle noen hva som hadde skjedd.

text:
Now, just for a few moments, if I'd speak just a little on God's written Word, well, the meeting would be a success even by the reading of His Word. Now, may the Lord add His blessings.
We're speaking now of Jesus in this great time of the resurrection. There was three people that He raised while He was on earth. He said, before He went to the grave of Lazarus, "I am the resurrection and life. He that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live. And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die." And He had the resurrection power in Him. He said, "You destroy this temple, and I'll raise it up again in three days."
Now, if He was the resurrection and life, but all the time in His ministry, He only raised up three people from the dead. Now, no doubt but He had many critics in that day that said, "Why didn't He raise up these?" And, "Why didn't he raise up this one?" And, "This priest just died, this holy man just died. Why didn't He raise up them?"
Jesus plainly stated that He only did what the Father told Him to do … what the Father showed Him in visions. Jesus saw visions, and only worked as God showed Him by visions only.
St. John 5:19, He said, "Verily, verily I say unto you the Son can do nothing in Himself, but what He sees the Father doing, that doeth the Son likewise."---just what the Father showed Him, and nothing else. Nothing could He do except, first, God reveal it.
So, three is a witness. For it's written in the law that three is a confirmation. In the mouth of two or three witnesses, let every word be established.
text_no:
La oss nå, i noen få øyeblikk, ta for oss Guds skrevne Ord. Selv bare ved å lese Hans Ord blir møtet vellykket. Må Herren legge Sine velsignelser til dette.
Vi snakker om Jesus i denne store tiden for oppstandelsen. Det finnes tre personer Han oppreiste mens Han var på jorden. Før Han gikk til graven til Lasarus, sa Han: "Jeg er oppstandelsen og livet. Den som tror på Meg, skal leve om han enn dør. Og hver den som lever og tror på Meg, skal aldri i evighet dø." Han hadde oppstandelseskraften i Seg. Han sa: "Ødelegg dette tempelet, og Jeg skal reise det opp igjen på tre dager."
Nå, hvis Han var oppstandelsen og livet, men i løpet av hele Sin tjeneste, oppreiste Han bare tre personer fra de døde. Uten tvil hadde Han mange kritikere som sa: "Hvorfor oppreiste Han ikke disse?" eller "Hvorfor oppreiste Han ikke denne?" og "Denne presten døde nettopp, denne hellige mannen døde nettopp. Hvorfor oppreiste Han ikke dem?"
Jesus sa tydelig at Han bare gjorde det Faderen fortalte Ham å gjøre, det Faderen viste Ham i syner. Jesus så syner og handlet kun som Gud viste Ham.
I Johannes 5:19 sier Han: "Sannelig, sannelig sier jeg dere: Sønnen kan ikke gjøre noe av Seg selv, men bare det Han ser Faderen gjøre. For det Faderen gjør, det gjør også Sønnen." Han gjorde kun det Faderen viste Ham, og ingenting annet. Ingenting kunne Han gjøre uten at Gud først åpenbarte det for Ham.
Så, tre vitner er en bekreftelse. For det står skrevet i loven at tre er en bekreftelse: "Ved to eller tre vitners utsagn skal enhver sak avgjøres."

text:
A Jewish man, here not long ago, said to me, said, "I can prove that Jesus was a thief."
And I said, "How?"
He said, "He went through the corn fields on the Sabbath day and eat corn that didn't belong to Him, proving He was a thief."
I said, "You're trying to compare the order of that day with this day." I said, "He said He was the Lord of the harvest." And He was. And another thing, I said, "Isn't it written in your law that if a man comes through the field, and he's hungry, he can eat what he wants to eat; but it's not lawful for him to pack anything away. So, He was within the law." That's right. Even to their own laws.
Oh, their hearts was just blinded so we could have a day of grace (that's all)---we Gentiles. And I'm so thankful for that. And I'm sure you all are.
text_no:
En jødisk mann sa nylig til meg: "Jeg kan bevise at Jesus var en tyv."
Jeg spurte: "Hvordan?"
Han svarte: "Han gikk gjennom kornåkrene på sabbaten og spiste korn som ikke tilhørte Ham, noe som beviser at Han var en tyv."
Jeg svarte: "Du prøver å sammenligne lovene fra den tid med våre dager." Jeg sa: "Han sa at Han var Herre over høsten." Og det var Han. Videre sa jeg: "Er det ikke skrevet i deres lov at hvis en mann går gjennom en åker og er sulten, kan han spise så mye han vil, men det er ikke lovlig å ta noe med seg. Så Han handlet innenfor loven." Det stemmer. Selv deres egne lover.
Å, deres hjerter var bare blindet så vi kunne ha en nådens dag -- vi hedninger. Og jeg er så takknemlig for det. Og jeg er sikker på at dere alle også er det.

text:
Now, the Son of God spent much time in prayer before the cross, and healing the people. We're just prior to our message tonight, He crossed all the way across the sea; went up on top of a mountain one evening after breaking bread and feeding five thousand. I wonder where He got the atoms … or, what atom He turned loose, rather, when He fed five thousand people with two little fishes and five loaves of bread … five little biscuits that some little child had.
Not only was the fish fish, but it was cooked fish, and cooked bread. He done something, no one knows yet, but He did it because He was the Lord of creation. And there, up on the mountain, He stood and watched the disciples as they was crossing the sea; the little boat tossed about and was just about ready to sink, looked like. And they seen someone coming. And here He come, walking on the sea.
text_no:
Guds Sønn tilbrakte mye tid i bønn før korset og helbredet mennesker. Like før budskapet vårt i kveld, krysset Han hele veien over sjøen; Han gikk opp på en fjelltopp en kveld etter å ha brutt brød og mettet fem tusen. Jeg undrer på hvilke atomer Han brukte da Han mettet fem tusen mennesker med to små fisker og fem brød ... fem små boller fra et lite barn.
Ikke bare var fisken ekte fisk, men den var også tilberedt, og brødet var bakt. Han gjorde noe ingen ennå forstår, men Han gjorde det fordi Han er skapelsens Herre. Der, oppe på fjellet, sto Han og så på disiplene mens de krysset sjøen; den lille båten ble kastet rundt og så ut til å synke. Disiplene så noen komme. Her kom Han, gående på sjøen.

text:
That's the way He does. Just about the time it gets the very darkest of hour, then Jesus comes along. We fret and worry, we Christians, and the baby gets sick, or something happens, or somebody don't show up. We're just so nervous and upset. We should just sit quiet. He's the one who commanded it. We're living under His dispensation, living under His grace. Just sit still; He'll be along after awhile. Don't worry. He'll open up a healing service somewhere, or you'll be guided to somebody who prays for the sick when you got sickness. If not, He'll just put it upon your heart so bad, you'll just kneel down in the room and ask Him about it yourself. That's right.
So, just remember, He works all things together for good.
text_no:
Slik gjør Han det. Akkurat når det blir som mørkest, kommer Jesus. Vi bekymrer oss ofte, vi kristne, når barnet blir sykt, eller noe annet skjer, eller noen ikke dukker opp. Vi blir nervøse og urolige. Vi burde bare sitte rolig. Det er Han som har befalt det. Vi lever under Hans dispensasjon og under Hans nåde. Bare sitt stille; Han kommer snart. Ikke bekymre deg. Han vil åpne en helbredelsestjeneste et sted, eller lede deg til noen som ber for de syke når du er syk. Hvis ikke, vil Han legge det så sterkt på hjertet ditt at du kneler ned og spør Ham selv. 
Bare husk, Han får alle ting til å virke sammen til det gode.

text:
He got in the ship, the storm stopped. He crossed that stormy sea that night. So, the call of one needy soul---a maniac---up in Gadara up there; was just so mean till he was just cutting himself with rocks. And they'd bind him with chains and he'd break them.
Now, that's the devil's power. Did you ever fool around with somebody that was a maniac? You ever seen somebody with double their power, it's a maniac, because they're completely anointed with Satan. And that satanic power gives them twice their strength.
Now, if a person completely surrendered to the devil will give him twice his strength, what will a man completely surrendered to God do? See. It'll make you so weak. Maybe you're crippled and can't stand up. But surrender yourself to God and new life and new strength comes in, and you'll go to walking away.
I've seen little people when their little limbs wasn't hardly that big---grown men and women eat up with cancer---rise to the floor; that hadn't sat up for months. So weak they couldn't have stood up any other way but under the power of God. And get up and walk around through the house, praising God, and get well.
What is it? Full surrendered to God. Amen. That's what it takes: a complete surrender.
text_no:
Han gikk om bord i skipet, og stormen stilnet. Han krysset det stormfulle havet den natten. Kallet fra en fortvilet sjel, en maniakk i Gadara, var så desperat at mannen skar seg selv med steiner. De bandt ham med lenker, men han brøt dem.
Dette er djevelens makt. Har du noen gang hatt å gjøre med en maniakk? Har du sett noen med dobbelt styrke? Det er en maniakk, helt fylt av Satan. Den sataniske makten gir dem dobbel styrke.
Hvis en person som er totalt overgitt til djevelen får dobbel styrke, hva vil skje med en mann som er totalt overgitt til Gud? Det vil gjøre deg så svak. Kanskje du er krøpling og ikke kan stå opp. Men overgir du deg til Gud, vil nytt liv og ny styrke komme inn, og du vil begynne å gå.
Jeg har sett små mennesker med tynne lemmer—voksne menn og kvinner oppspist av kreft—reise seg fra gulvet etter å ha ligget i månedsvis. Så svake at de ikke kunne ha stått opp på noen annen måte enn under Guds kraft. Og de reiste seg og gikk rundt i huset, priset Gud, og ble friske.
Hva er det? Full overgivelse til Gud. Amen. Det er det som kreves: en fullstendig overgivelse.

text:
And notice, when that man, that maniac, was so completely surrendered to Satan, Satan used his tongue. And he said, "We are Legion."
Jesus said, "Who are you?"
And the man couldn't even speak himself. Satan spoke through him: "We are Legion, for we are many."
Jesus said, "Hold you peace, but come out of him."
And when that maniac was healed, those devils went out and went into some hogs---about two thousand hogs---and they come out. Because it was going to cost them a little something to have the revival, they asked Jesus to leave the country. They felt more at home with the devil and the hogs than they did with the lovely Jesus. That's right. And they said … they said, "Oh, leave our country. My, You're too much of a spiritualist for us. We can't stand that. Now, if You're going to get people healed around here, such stuff as that, You get out of our country."
Well, He never stays where He's not welcome, so He went on back. But there was one needy soul that He crossed the stormy seas to get to, and He's the same Jesus tonight. He'd come from glory to one needy soul sitting in this humble church tonight. Same One. He'll every time come to where He's wanted, but never come where He's doubted.
So, if you're doubting Him, just remember: He'll never be at your door, see. But if you love Him and want Him, He'll come any time you call for Him. He's ready to come.
text_no:
Legg merke til hvordan da mannen, den besatte, var fullstendig overgitt til Satan, brukte Satan hans tunge. Satan sa: "Vi er Legion."
Jesus spurte: "Hvem er du?"
Mannen kunne ikke snakke selv. Satan svarte gjennom ham: "Vi er Legion, for vi er mange."
Jesus sa: "Hold fred og kom ut av ham."
Da den besatte ble helbredet, gikk demonene ut og inn i noen griser—omtrent to tusen griser—og de kom ut. Fordi det ville koste dem noe å ha vekkelsen, ba de Jesus forlate landet. De følte seg mer hjemme med djevelen og grisene enn med den elskelige Jesus. Det stemmer. De sa: "Oh, forlat vårt land. Du er for spirituell for oss. Vi tåler det ikke. Hvis Du skal helbrede folk her, forlat landet vårt."
Jesus blir aldri der Han ikke er velkommen, så Han dro videre. Men det var én nødende sjel som Han krysset de stormfulle havene for å nå, og Han er den samme Jesus i kveld. Han ville komme fra herlighet til én nødende sjel som sitter i denne ydmyke menigheten i kveld. Den samme. Han kommer alltid der Han er ønsket, men aldri der Han er tvilt på.
Så, hvis du tviler på Ham, husk: Han vil aldri være ved din dør, se. Men hvis du elsker Ham og ønsker Ham, vil Han komme når som helst du kaller på Ham. Han er klar til å komme.

text:
Now, we notice we're taking this little fellow, Jairus. Many people kind of criticize Jairus, that had this daughter that died. He was a priest at the temple, or one of the great men, chief men, around the temple. But I believe he was kind of a secret believer. I believe down in his heart he really believed Jesus, but he had just… he'd got himself mixed up with the wrong crowd.
And you know what? I find that everywhere tonight. Sometimes in a great, classic society of people, there are secret believers up there. Great multi-millionaires, and yet, they're secret believers, but they're just kind of afraid to associate themselves with this class of people. They try to come by night and knock at the door. All right.
Nicodemus was one of those types. Come around, said, "Now Master, I know that You're not so popular. Oh, there's a bunch of these holy rollers following You, or something, or common people. That's what they claim that You associate with: the common and lower class of people. But we in the temple know that You're a teacher come from God, for no man could do the things that You did without God being with him." There's a true confession, see. But he'd associated himself in the wrong class.
And there's many of those people in Louisville, Kentucky, tonight, perhaps lots sitting right here. That's right. You've just got yourself up into a big church somewhere, got the biggest name there is around the country. You think that gets you somewhere. It'll cause you to lose your soul. It'll do that for you.
Just humble yourself down. Always remember: The way down is up with God, see. "He that humbles himself, God will exalt. He that exalts himself shall be brought abase," said Jesus. So just humble down, come on down, get it the same way the rest of them does.
text_no:
La oss se nærmere på denne lille karen, Jairus. Mange kritiserer Jairus, som hadde en datter som døde. Han var en prest ved tempelet, eller en av de store mennene, sjefene, rundt tempelet. Men jeg tror han var en slags hemmelig troende. Jeg tror inderst inne at han virkelig trodde på Jesus, men han hadde bare… han hadde rotet seg opp i feil selskap. 
Og vet du hva? Jeg finner det overalt i kveld. Noen ganger i et stort, klassisk samfunn av mennesker, finnes det hemmelige troende der oppe. Store multimillionærer, men likevel hemmelige troende, men de er bare litt redde for å assosiere seg med denne typen mennesker. De prøver å komme om natten og banke på døren. 
Nikodemus var en av disse typene. Han kom og sa: "Mester, jeg vet at Du ikke er så populær. Å, det er en gjeng av disse hellige rullene som følger Deg, eller noe, eller vanlige folk. Det er det de hevder at Du omgås: de vanlige og lavere klasser av folk. Men vi i tempelet vet at Du er en lærer send fra Gud, for ingen kunne gjøre de tingene Du gjorde uten at Gud var med Ham." Det er en sann bekjennelse, ser du. Men han hadde assosiert seg med feil gruppe. 
Og det er mange av den typen i Louisville, Kentucky, i kveld, kanskje mange sitter her akkurat nå. Det stemmer. Du har plassert deg i en stor menighet et sted, fått det største navnet som finnes rundt i landet. Du tror det gir deg noe. Det kan føre til at du mister din sjel. Det vil det gjøre. 
Bare ydmyk deg. Husk alltid: Veien ned er opp med Gud, ser du. "Den som ydmyker seg, vil Gud opphøye. Den som opphøyer seg selv, skal bli fornedret," sa Jesus. Så bare ydmyk deg, kom ned, og få det på samme måte som resten av dem.

text:
One night I had an altar call at the tabernacle many years ago. There was a fine young fellow who was a Bible teacher at a class down there in a certain church. There was a lady down at the altar just crying and going on. He said, "Oh, my." He said, "Do you mean she has to do that?"
I said, "I didn't say she had to do it, but she was doing it."
He said, "Well, I'll never get it if that's what it takes."
I said, "Don't worry. You won't."
Naaman said, "Isn't the waters up here in my country a lot better than they are down there?" But that was the Word of the Lord. He didn't have to go dip, but he went back with his leprosy. That's right. So it was when he obeyed God's Word, he got rid of his leprosy.
So, that's what God sometimes makes us do things we don't want to do sometimes.
It was mighty hard for me in a few things, too, but He had to take the starch all out of me. He knows how to do it, too, in a real way.
text_no:
For mange år siden holdt jeg en alterkall på tabernaklet en kveld. En ung mann, som underviste i Bibelen i en bestemt menighet, var til stede. En kvinne ved alteret gråt og var opprørt. Han sa: "Å, herregud. Mener du at hun må gjøre det?"
Jeg svarte: "Jeg sa ikke at hun må gjøre det, men hun gjør det."
Han sa: "Vel, jeg vil aldri oppnå det hvis det er det som trengs."
Jeg svarte: "Ikke bekymre deg. Det vil du ikke."
Naaman sa: "Er ikke vannet i mitt land mye bedre enn der nede?" Men det var Herrens Ord. Han trengte ikke å dykke, men han ville ha forlatt med sin spedalskhet. Det er riktig. Så da han adlød Guds Ord, ble han kvitt spedalskheten.
Gud får oss noen ganger til å gjøre ting vi ikke ønsker å gjøre. Det var også veldig vanskelig for meg å overkomme noen ting, men Han måtte ta all stoltheten ut av meg. Han vet hvordan Han skal gjøre det på en virkelig måte.

text:
And so, I can notice, Jairus had a social standing among the people. And he'd get out that day… And, no doubt, He was the talk of the country. "That fanatic that's preaching divine healing. That Jesus of Nazareth. Nonsense. The days of miracles is past. Since our fathers in the wilderness, such things hasn't taken place. The man is a spiritualist. He's a mind-reader. Look at Him standing there, look out over the audience and tell different people different things they're thinking about. Hah! Away with such stuff." That's what they'd say.
Little Jairus sitting back there, "I believe Him, but I can't say nothing. I've got a prestige I've got to hold up."
All right. And the first thing you know, he was walking around one day, and every time anybody'd go talking about Jesus, he'd kind of keep quiet. He didn't want to say too much about it, because he was a secret believer.
Then one day his little girl took sick. You know God knows just how to work on you sometimes.
text_no:
Så jeg legger merke til at Jairus hadde en sosial status blant folket. Den dagen, uten tvil, var temaet på alles lepper: "Den fanatikeren som forkynner guddommelig helbredelse. Jesus fra Nasaret. Tull. Miraklenes tid er forbi. Siden våre fedre var i ørkenen, har slike ting ikke skjedd. Mannen er en spiritualist. Han er en tankeleser. Se på Ham der Han står og ser ut over publikum og forteller forskjellige mennesker ulike ting de tenker på. Ha! Bort med slikt tull." Det var slik de snakket.
Lille Jairus satt der og tenkte, "Jeg tror på Ham, men jeg kan ikke si noe. Jeg har en prestisje jeg må opprettholde."
Og så, en dag gikk han rundt, og hver gang noen snakket om Jesus, holdt han seg litt stille. Han ville ikke si for mye om det, for han var en hemmelig troende.
En dag ble hans lille datter syk. Du vet, Gud vet akkurat hvordan Han skal jobbe med deg noen ganger.

text:
His little girl got sick. So, well, I guess the reasonable thing to do, he probably called in the doctor. The doctor said, "Well, we'll give her some of these homeopathic drugs," or whatever they was practicing with, "and she'll be all right in a few days."
So, he give her the drugs and she got worse. And they called the doctor maybe two or three times and she continually gotten worse. Then, now, the blow comes. The doctor walks out the door with Jairus and says, "Hate to tell you here, fellow, but your little girl's at the point of death. She's going to die. There's nothing in the world can help her. All of my medical knowledge has… I've given every bit to you, and still the little girl is going to die. There's nothing can be done. She's just going to be called home by Jehovah. So, might as well get ready and get the grave and her shroud and everything ready, because she's going to die."
Oh, I could imagine seeing little Jairus' heart shaking like that. Way down deep under there was a little thought, "Wonder where Jesus is."
Sometimes it'd have to get about like that at your house to make you wonder where Jesus is … to put that stuff you're doing, and come and find where He's at.
text_no:
Hans lille jente ble syk. Så, jeg antar at den fornuftige tingen å gjøre var å tilkalle en lege. Legen sa: "Vel, vi gir henne noen av disse homeopatiske medisinene," eller hva de nå enn brukte, "og hun vil bli frisk om noen dager."
Så, han ga henne medisinene, men hun ble verre. De tilkalte legen kanskje to eller tre ganger, og hun ble stadig verre. Så kom det avgjørende øyeblikket. Legen gikk ut døren med Jairus og sa: "Jeg hater å fortelle deg dette, men din lille jente er døden nær. Hun kommer til å dø. Det finnes ingenting i verden som kan hjelpe henne. All min medisinske kunnskap har blitt brukt, og likevel vil den lille jenta dø. Ingenting kan gjøres. Hun vil bli kalt hjem til Jehova. Så det beste er å gjøre seg klar og forberede graven og hennes svøpe, for hun kommer til å dø."
Åh, jeg kan forestille meg at Jairus' hjerte rystet ved denne beskjeden. Dypt der inne var det en liten tanke: "Lurer på hvor Jesus er."
Noen ganger må situasjonen bli så desperat hjemme hos deg før du begynner å lure på hvor Jesus er … før du legger bort alt annet og søker etter hvor Han er.

text:
Oh, as soon as the doctor left, I can hear him come over to his wife and say, "Dear, you know, all along, way down in my heart…"
She said, "I know what you're talking about, honey. You're thinking about where is that fellow that's praying for the sick, called Jesus of Nazareth."
"Yeah, that… You know, honey, really, I've noticed you searching the Scriptures every night. I believe you kind of believed on Him. You know what? I believed on Him, too."
"Well, wife, bless your heart. Let's go find Him."
Somebody must've come told him that Jesus was coming. He was coming across the sea then, oaring in his little boat, you know, coming across the river … the sea … little lake there. And as He come across, you know, they told him Jesus was coming… Faith cometh by hearing, (Is that right?) hearing of the Word. So somebody told him that Jesus was coming over to have a one-day campaign, maybe over in his country.
text_no:
Så snart legen gikk, hørte jeg ham gå bort til sin kone og si: "Kjære, du vet, dypt inni meg har jeg alltid…"
Hun svarte: "Jeg vet hva du mener, kjære. Du tenker på hvor den mannen som ber for de syke, kalt Jesus fra Nasaret, er."
"Ja, akkurat det… Du vet, kjære, jeg har lagt merke til at du har søkt i Skriftene hver kveld. Jeg tror at du har trodd på Ham. Vet du hva? Jeg har også trodd på Ham."
"Vel, kjære, velsigne deg. La oss gå og finne Ham."
Noen må ha fortalt ham at Jesus kom. Han kom over sjøen, roende i sin lille båt, over elven… sjøen… den lille innsjøen der. Og mens Han kom over, vet du, de fortalte ham at Jesus var på vei… Troen kommer av å høre, (Stemmer det?), høre Ordet. Så noen fortalte ham at Jesus ville komme for å ha en dags kampanje, kanskje i hans land.

text:
So, right quick, I can see him get his coat on and pull his little turban down over his head and get started. I hear some of his church members standing there saying, "Where are you going, Jairus?"
And I hear them say, "You know what? They tell me that that holy roller's coming down there. He's praying for the sick. I bet He's going down there."
I can hear the deacons walk out and say, "Now, just a moment, Jairus. Now, you know every time we have the conference, we elect a new pastor, if we desire. Now, we don't want any disgrace brought upon our church. Now, if you're going to have any of that divine healing stuff around here, I want you to know right now, that we're not going to stand for it."
Brother, the time had come for action. Jairus had to have some action. The time may strike you for action. And if you want to know, I think the time for Louisville now is for action. That's right. The time for the church, it's time for action. They've played around and throwed this off at the corner, and brushed off of this, and called it this, that. Let's act. Amen. Louisville, stand on your feet, claim your God-given privileges.
And, it's time for action. Not, "Well, I tell you, I'll sit back and see if I… I'll go tonight." Like Nicodemus come by night. But, Jesus had a need right … I mean, Jairus had a need right then.
And, brother, we got a need right now, and that's, get to Him. Not wait till the next meeting, wait and see if somebody else comes and preaches the same thing. We got a need now. Let's press forward to get Him. The midnight hour is here.
text_no:
Så, umiddelbart, ser jeg ham ta på seg jakken, trekke den lille turbanen ned over hodet og starte av gårde. Jeg hører noen av menighetens medlemmer stå der og si: "Hvor skal du, Jairus?"
Jeg hører dem si: "Vet du hva? De sier den hellige rulleren kommer der nedover. Han ber for de syke. Jeg vedder på at han skal dit."
Jeg kan høre diakonene komme ut og si: "Vent litt, Jairus. Du vet at hver gang vi har konferanse, kan vi velge en ny pastor, om vi ønsker. Vi vil ikke at det skal bringes vanære over vår menighet. Hvis du skal ha noe av det med guddommelig helbredelse her, vil vi at du skal vite nå, at vi ikke vil akseptere det."
Bror, tiden var inne for handling. Jairus måtte handle. Tiden kan også komme for deg, for handling. Og hvis du vil vite det, mener jeg at tiden for Louisville er nå. Det er riktig. Tiden for menigheten, det er tid for handling. De har lekt rundt og kastet dette til side, og børstet dette bort, og kalt det dette eller hint. La oss handle. Amen. Louisville, reis dere og krev deres Gud-gitte privilegier.
Det er tid for handling. Ikke: "Vel, jeg skal se om jeg… jeg går i kveld." Som Nikodemus kom om natten. Men Jesus hadde et behov akkurat … Jeg mener, Jairus hadde et behov akkurat da.
Og, bror, vi har et behov akkurat nå, og det er å nå Ham. Ikke vent til neste møte, vent og se om noen andre kommer og preker det samme. Vi har et behov nå. La oss presse frem for å nå Ham. Midnattstimen er her.

text:
The little girl was at the point of death; doctors had failed. Now, I can hear them giving him a good sharp talking to; and the board of trustees, and all, going over him. But he had a need: A loved one was laying at the point of death, so he must get to Jesus.
Here he starts on. About that time, the little old boat stopped down there and Jesus got out. And a bunch of spectators standing on the bank. The priests standing back up there, said, "Mm-hmm. There He is. Look what kind of a crowd He's got now. There comes that bunch of illiterate, unlearned, uncouth people coming up off the banks down there. Those fishermen walking down there. See what kind of a crowd He associates with? The birds of a feather, that's it." … standing there, criticizing.
text_no:
Den lille jenta var på dødens rand; legene hadde mislyktes. Nå kan jeg høre dem gi ham en skarp tilrettevisning; og styret, alle sammen, gikk gjennom ham. Men han hadde et behov: en kjær var på dødens rand, så han måtte komme seg til Jesus.
Her starter han. Omtrent på den tiden stoppet den lille båten der nede, og Jesus gikk ut. En flokk tilskuere sto på bredden. Prestene som sto lenger bak, sa: "Mm-hmm. Der er Han. Se hva slags folkemengde Han har nå. Der kommer den gjengen med uvitende, ulærte, uhøflige mennesker opp fra bredden der nede. De fiskerne som kommer ned der. Ser du hva slags folk Han omgås med? Likesinnede flokker seg sammen." … som sto der og kritiserte.

text:
Way back up on the hill, I can see a little woman doing her knitting. She had spent all of her money for the doctors and they couldn't do her no good. She had a blood issue for several years. She looked down; she said, "Isn't it strange, I was just dreaming about Him last night," perhaps. "And I believe if I could only get to that Man and touch His garment, I wouldn't have to get in the prayer line. He'd just turn around and tell me it was over if I could just get close enough to Him." That's the way to do it. That's just it. "If I could just get close enough to Him, that's all I need."
Well, I see her walk down here. There stands her pastor. She looks up. "Now, where are you going? I suppose then you're going down to that meeting. Well, I want to let you know this: You know what the orders are around here. Any person associates with that tribe, they're given their letter."
Said, "You can just have it." Here she goes on, right on down. She had to get to Jesus.
And as soon as she got there, I can see her standing before a big bunch, look down there. "Well, I'm surprised at you. A woman that the doctors has turned down---they can't do nothing for ---and then you… And a woman with a reputation that you've got, as a good woman, the reputation and the prestige that you've got amongst the people; and here you are associating yourself with that bunch of holy rollers. I never seen such, the very audacity. Hmm. The days of miracles is past."
I hear her turn around and say, "It might be for you, but not for me. Just let me get a little closer to Him." She's pressing on. She's got to get up there.
text_no:
På veien opp bakken kan jeg se en liten kvinne som sitter og strikker. Hun hadde brukt alle pengene sine på leger som ikke kunne hjelpe henne. Hun hadde hatt blødninger i flere år. Hun så ned og sa: "Er det ikke merkelig, jeg drømte om Ham i går natt," kanskje. "Og jeg tror at hvis jeg bare kunne komme til den Mannen og røre ved Hans klær, så ville jeg ikke trenge å stå i bønnekøen. Han ville bare snu seg og fortelle meg at det var over hvis jeg bare kunne komme nær nok til Ham." Det er slik det skal gjøres. Det er akkurat det. "Hvis jeg bare kunne komme nær nok til Ham, det er alt jeg trenger."
Vel, jeg ser henne gå ned hit. Der står hennes pastor. Hun ser opp. "Nå, hvor er du på vei? Jeg antar at du skal ned til det møtet. Vel, jeg vil at du skal vite dette: Du vet hvilke regler som gjelder her. Enhver person som forbindes med den gruppen, får sitt medlemsbrev tilbake."
Hun svarte: "Du kan bare beholde det." Så gikk hun videre. Hun måtte komme til Jesus.
Og så snart hun kom dit, ser jeg henne stå foran en stor folkemengde og se nedover. "Vel, jeg er overrasket over deg. En kvinne som legene har gitt opp --- de kan ikke gjøre noe for deg --- og så du... Og en kvinne med ditt gode rykte, prestisjen du har blant folket; og her er du, som forbinder deg med den gruppen av hellighetsfolk. Jeg har aldri sett maken til frekkhet. Hmm. Miraklenes tid er forbi."
Jeg hører henne svare: "Det kan kanskje være for deg, men ikke for meg. Bare la meg komme litt nærmere Ham." Hun presser på. Hun må komme seg opp dit.

text:
And after a while some fellow says, "I'll just tell you right now, Aunt Lydia, I'm just not going to let you do it. We're just going to stand here and not let you do it."
I can see her just stoop right down, walk right between his legs and go right on through. She's determined to get to Him regardless of the cost, regardless of the price.
That's the way we ought to be. No matter what says this or what side's that, God said it in His Word and that settles it for me. God said so, that's just … that's all of it. I believe it.
So here she comes. And she gets down there, and she seen all the people stand up around and putting their arms around Him. She just slipped along behind Him and touched His garment like that. And she thought, Oh, Lord, that just satisfies me. I got to the meeting, so that's good enough to me.
All at once, there was something… He had some way of knowing things. He turned around, said, "Who touched Me?" Looked around over the audience till He found where she was. Said, "Thy faith has saved thee. Your blood issue has done stopped now."
Why, He's the same tonight---just the same Jesus. If He's risen from the dead, He said, "The things that I do shall you also." Is that right? And He said, "If you believe," that's all you had to do. All right. And she was healed.
text_no:
Og etter en stund sier en fyr, "Jeg skal bare si deg nå, Tante Lydia, jeg lar deg ikke gjøre det. Vi kommer bare til å stå her og hindre deg."
Jeg kan se henne bøye seg ned, gå rett mellom beina hans og gå rett igjennom. Hun er bestemt på å nå frem til Ham uansett kostnad.
Det er slik vi bør være. Uansett hva andre sier, Gud sa det i Hans Ord, og det holder for meg. Gud sa det, og det er nok. Jeg tror det.
Så her kommer hun. Hun kommer seg frem og ser alle menneskene stå rundt og holde armene rundt Ham. Hun bare sniker seg forbi og rører ved kanten av kappen Hans. Hun tenker, “Å Herre, det er nok for meg. Jeg kom meg til møtet, og det er godt nok for meg.”
Plutselig var det noe … Han hadde en måte å vite ting på. Han snudde seg rundt og sa, "Hvem rørte ved Meg?" Han så rundt i forsamlingen til Han fant henne. Han sa, "Din tro har frelst deg. Blodsykdommen din er stoppet nå."
Han er den samme i kveld---den samme Jesus. Hvis Han er oppstanden fra de døde, sa Han, "De tingene Jeg gjør, skal dere også gjøre." Er det riktig? Og Han sa, "Hvis dere tror," det er alt dere trenger å gjøre. Okei. Og hun ble helbredet.

text:
And then about that time, I see this little priest pushing through the crowd. Some of them said, "Well, looky there. Well, there's Jairus, the one that just got his degree last year. Well, you mean to tell me he just got his bachelor's degree and here he is down there fooling around with that group now."
Why was he down there? He had a need. That's right. He had a hungry heart. God was going to show him all about these things. So, he slips right up to where Jesus was and he said, "Lord, my little girl is at the point of death. Come lay Your hands on her, and she'll get well."
My, I just like that. Oh, when he'd seen he got close enough to Him that he could attract His attention---if he could attract the attention of the Man… God was already dealing with his heart, so if he could attract the attention of the Man, he'd get what he wanted, because he knowed that God was in His Son.
text_no:
Omtrent på denne tiden ser jeg en liten prest som presser seg gjennom mengden. Noen av dem sa: "Se der. Der er Jairus, han som akkurat fikk sin grad i fjor. Mener du å fortelle meg at han nettopp fikk sin bachelorgrad, og her er han nå og roter rundt med den gruppen der?"
Hvorfor var han der? Han hadde et behov. Det stemmer. Han hadde et sultent hjerte. Gud skulle vise ham alt om disse tingene. Så han smyger seg opp til der Jesus var og sier: "Herre, min lille datter er i ferd med å dø. Kom og legg Dine hender på henne, så vil hun bli frisk."
Å, jeg likte det. Når han så at han var nær nok til Jesus til å få Hans oppmerksomhet—hvis han kunne tiltrekke seg oppmerksomheten til Mannen… Gud arbeidet allerede med hans hjerte, så hvis han kunne fange oppmerksomheten til Mannen, ville han få det han ønsket, fordi han visste at Gud var i Hans Sønn.

text:
I like the Shunammite woman there on that, when her baby had died and was laying in the prophet's chambers. She said, "Saddle a mule and go forward. Don't you stop till I bid you."
And when she come… God don't always reveal to His servants everything that goes on; just as He wants to reveal it. And so the woman drove up real quick… We'll leave our story here a moment. The woman got up real quick. And Elisha got up, looked over, come out of his cave and looked around there. And he said, to Gehazi, said, "Here comes that Shunammite." Said, "Her heart's troubled and I don't even know what's wrong with her."
So he come up. Still God didn't tell him what was wrong. He said, "Is all well with thee? Is all well with thy husband? Is all well with thy child?"
I like this: She said, "All is well with me, and all is well with my husband, and all is well with the child." And the baby laying, dead.
Well, what? She had come to the man of God. It was that same man of God give her the baby by a promise. And she knowed that God was in His prophet. And if she could ever get to the prophet, the prophet could let her know why God taken the baby.
She said, "All now is well." Then she fell down and began to tell him about that baby. And you know the story, how he went and laid his body on the baby … come to life.
text_no:
Jeg liker Shunamitt-kvinnen i denne historien, når hennes barn hadde dødd og lå i profetens kammer. Hun sa: "Sal opp et esel og gå fremover. Ikke stopp før jeg ber deg."
Da hun kom… Gud åpenbarer ikke alltid alt for sine tjenere; bare så mye Han ønsker å åpenbare. Så kvinnen skyndte seg av sted… Vi lar historien stå her et øyeblikk. Kvinnen reiste seg raskt. Elisja kom ut av hulen sin, kikket rundt og sa til Gehazi: "Her kommer Shunamitt-kvinnen. Hennes hjerte er bekymret, og jeg vet ikke engang hva som er galt."
Da hun kom opp, hadde Gud fortsatt ikke åpenbart hva som var galt. Han sa: "Er alt vel med deg? Er alt vel med din mann? Er alt vel med ditt barn?"
Jeg liker dette: Hun sa, "Alt er vel med meg, alt er vel med min mann, og alt er vel med barnet." Selv om barnet lå dødt.
Hva så? Hun hadde kommet til Guds mann. Det var den samme Guds mann som hadde gitt henne barnet ved et løfte. Og hun visste at Gud var i Hans profet, og hvis hun kunne få tak i profeten, kunne profeten fortelle henne hvorfor Gud hadde tatt barnet.
Hun sa, "Alt er vel nå." Så falt hun ned og begynte å fortelle ham om barnet. Og dere kjenner historien, hvordan han gikk og la sin kropp på barnet, som da kom til liv.

text:
Little Jairus had worked through the crowd till he got down there. And he said, "Now, all is well. I finally got to Him. I got away from the pastors, I got away from the bishop, I got away from the pope, and I got away from all of them. And now, I've got down from all the church members, and through all the societies and everything else, now. Now I've got down to where I can really worship Him."
He said, "Lord, I'm so glad to be here. And my little girl is laying at the point of death. Will You come, lay Your hands on her?"
Now watch. His faith was laying on hands, but Peter's wasn't like that. When he was on the stormy sea the night before, and Jesus was standing out there, he said, "Lord, if it be You, bid me to come to You on the water. Just ask me to come."
Jesus said, "Come on." You know, "Seek and ye shall find. Ask and it'll be given to you."
So, Peter steps down out of the boat and away he went.
text_no:
Lille Jairus hadde kjempet seg gjennom folkemengden til han endelig nådde fram. Han sa, "Nå er alt vel. Jeg kom meg endelig til Ham. Jeg unngikk pastorene, biskopen, paven og alle andre. Nå har jeg kommet forbi menighetsmedlemmene, alle organisasjonene og alt annet. Nå kan jeg virkelig tilbe Ham."
Han sa, "Herre, jeg er så glad for å være her. Min lille jente ligger for døden. Vil Du komme og legge Dine hender på henne?"
Legg merke til dette: Hans tro var basert på håndspåleggelse, men Peter sin var ikke slik. Da han var på det stormfulle havet natten før og Jesus stod der, sa han, "Herre, hvis det er Deg, kall meg til Deg på vannet. Bare be meg om å komme."
Jesus sa, "Kom." Du vet, "Søk, og dere skal finne. Be, og dere skal få."
Så Peter steg ut av båten og gikk mot Ham.

text:
The priests: When Joshua gave the commission to cross over the sea … over the river, rather, their faith was when their feet touched the water. As soon as the soles of their feet touched the water, they knew the sea would drive back. That's where their faith was.
To the Roman centurion: He said, "I'm not worthy that You should come under my roof. Just speak the word." I like that. Look at this: He said, "I'm a man under authority. Now I say to this man, 'Do this.' and he does it. And I say to this man, 'Go.' and he goes; this man 'Come.' and he comes. And know what I have… All under me has to obey me."
Now, watch. He recognized that Jesus Christ had sickness under his control. He said, "You just speak the word (That's all You have to do.), and the sickness of my servant will obey what You say do."
Give us that kind of faith here, tonight, and you'll see something happen. That's right. "Just speak the word. Whatever You say, that's the way it'll be. Just say the word."
And He said, "Thy son liveth."
And so the next day, when he went down about eleven o'clock, he was coming near home. He'd traveled all that day, and that night. And the next day he come in. Some of his servants met him, said, "Thy son liveth."
He said, "What time did he start to begin to get well."
Said, "Yesterday at about eleven o'clock the fever left him."
The man said, "That's just when He told me." That's right. The son begin to mend at that time.
text_no:
Prestene: Da Josva ga oppdraget om å krysse sjøen … eller rettere sagt, elven, var deres tro slik at vannet ville trekke seg tilbake så snart føttene deres rørte vannet. Det var der troen deres lå.
Til den romerske offiseren: Han sa, "Jeg er ikke verdig at Du skal komme inn under mitt tak. Bare si Ordet." Jeg liker det. Se på dette: Han sa, "Jeg er en mann med autoritet. Jeg sier til denne mannen, 'Gjør dette,' og han gjør det. Og jeg sier til denne mannen, 'Gå,' og han går; og til denne mannen, 'Kom,' og han kommer. Og jeg vet … Alt under meg må adlyde meg."
Nå, se her. Han innså at Jesus Kristus hadde sykdommen under Sin kontroll. Han sa, "Bare Si Ordet (Det er alt Du trenger å gjøre.), og sykdommen til min tjener vil adlyde det Du sier."
Gi oss den typen tro her i kveld, og du vil se noe skje. Det er sant. "Bare Si Ordet. Uansett hva Du sier, slik vil det bli. Bare Si Ordet."
Og Han sa, "Din sønn lever."
Så neste dag, da han reiste hjemover om lag klokken elleve, hadde han reist hele dagen og natten. Da han kom nær hjemmet, møtte noen av tjenerne ham og sa: "Din sønn lever."
Han spurte, "Når begynte han å bli frisk?"
De svarte, "I går rundt klokken elleve forsvant feberen."
Mannen sa, "Det var akkurat da Han sa det til meg." Det stemmer. Sønnen begynte å bli bedre på det tidspunktet.

text:
Now, Jairus wanted Him to lay hands on. Said, "Come lay Your hands on my daughter, and she'll get well."
Jesus said, "I'll go."
And He started on down there, because He knowed if a man's faith … whether He had a vision or not. Jesus had done said that He did nothing till the Father showed Him. Perhaps there's no vision of it, but yet, He knew the man's faith was sufficient to do the work.
The woman that touched Him back there: He never said, "I healed you." He said, "Thy faith has saved thee."
And He took his fingers… Two blind men followed Him one day in the street. Said, "Lord have mercy on us." He'd had no vision about those men. So He went on and left them alone. They cried after Him till He went into a house. They came into the house and said, "Have mercy on us, Lord."
Now watch. He touched their eyes, and said, "Now, according to your faith, be it unto you," see. And they had it. Their eyes come open, see. "According to your faith." But now, what He did Himself is what the Father showed Him to do. But He touched their eyes. They touched His garment and they done everything like that. That was their own faith. "Thy faith has saved thee."
text_no:
Jairus ønsket at Jesus skulle legge hendene på datteren hans. Han sa: "Kom, legg hendene på datteren min, så blir hun frisk."
Jesus svarte: "Jeg kommer."
Han begynte å gå dit, fordi Han visste at en manns tro, enten det var en visjon eller ikke, var tilstrekkelig. Jesus hadde allerede sagt at Han ikke gjorde noe uten at Faren viste Ham det. Selv om det kanskje ikke var noen visjon, visste Jesus at mannens tro var sterk nok til å få arbeidet gjort.
Kvinnen som rørte ved Jesus: Han sa aldri, "Jeg helbredet deg." Han sa, "Din tro har frelst deg."
En dag fulgte to blinde menn etter Ham på gaten og ropte: "Herre, ha barmhjertighet med oss." Jesus hadde ingen visjon om disse mennene, så Han ignorerte dem. De ropte etter Ham helt til Han gikk inn i et hus. Mennene fulgte etter og sa: "Ha barmhjertighet med oss, Herre."
Legg merke til dette: Jesus rørte ved øynene deres og sa: "La det skje etter deres tro." Og de ble helbredet. Øynene deres åpnet seg, som Han sa, "etter deres tro." Hva Jesus selv gjorde, var det som Faren viste Ham. Men når Han rørte ved deres øyne eller de rørte ved Hans kappe, var det deres egen tro som gjorde dem friske. "Din tro har frelst deg."

text:
So, here comes the man now. Jesus going with little Jairus to his daughter, sick. Now, they're going along. I can see Jairus walking along now. All the priests looking at Him and said, "Ah, so that's what you choose."
"Yup. This is it." … walking right along. He got close to Jesus. He'd had a change of heart. And every man that really gets close to Jesus can never be the same any more. No. You can't associate with that old dry, formal religions any more. You've got something in you that changes you and makes you different, if you ever come close… Now, you can go close to the church and do anything, but you can't come close to Jesus and be the same any more.
text_no:
Så, her kommer mannen nå. Jesus er på vei med lille Jairus til hans syke datter. De går sammen. Jeg kan se Jairus vandre ved siden av Ham nå. Alle prestene ser på Jesus og sier: "Ah, så det er det du velger."
"Ja. Dette er det." ... De går videre. Jairus kom nær Jesus; han hadde fått en forandring i hjertet. Enhver mann som virkelig kommer nær Jesus, kan aldri bli den samme igjen. Nei. Du kan ikke assosiere deg med den gamle, tørre, formelle religionen lenger. Det er noe inni deg som forandrer deg og gjør deg annerledes når du virkelig kommer nær Ham… Nå kan du komme nær Menigheten og gjøre hva som helst, men du kan ikke komme nær Jesus og forbli den samme.

text:
So, Jairus walking right along, his little chest throwed out, you know, like that. "Yes, sure. I'm glad to be walking by Him. Yes, sir. This is my salvation, now." Yes, sir.
Then the great stroke come. Here come a man with ashes on his head, as the Oriental type of mourning. And said, "Don't trouble him any more. Your daughter is already dead." That was the midnight blow.
I can see little Jairus' tears come in his eyes. His little heart begin to quiver. He looked around. I can see Jesus, with those sacred eyes looked and said, "Fear not. Only believe." That's enough. He pressed a little smile across his lips, and he started on.
"Well, I know what He says… He's anointed God. I know what He says is all right." So, was going on, he knew it would be all right, see. He knew something was all right.
When they got to the house, here they was screaming and going on out in the yard, "Oh, she's dead, she's gone. We've embalmed her body. She's laying on the couch, and she's dead, she's dead, the poor little thing."
Jesus started to go in, all that carrying on going on like that. He said, "Give peace. Keep still a little bit." He said, "The girl's not dead; she's only asleep."
And they laughed at him. Do you know what He done? When they laughed at God's Word, He put them out. That's a good thing to do. Don't you believe that? That's right. He put them out.
text_no:
Jairus gikk stolt ved siden av Jesus, med brystet spent fram. "Ja, jeg er glad for å gå ved Hans side. Dette er min frelse," tenkte han. Så kom det store slaget. En mann med aske på hodet, i typisk orientalsk sorg, nærmet seg og sa: "Ikke bry Ham mer. Din datter er allerede død." Dette var slaget ved midnatt.
Jeg kan se tårene i Jairus’ øyne. Hjertet hans begynner å skjelve. Han ser rundt seg. Jeg ser for meg Jesus, med sine hellige øyne, si: "Frykt ikke. Bare tro." Det var nok. Jairus smiler svakt og fortsetter.
"Jeg vet hva Han sier… Han er salvet av Gud. Jeg vet alt Han sier er rett." Så han fortsatte, sikker på at alt ville ordne seg. 
Da de kom til huset, var det skrik og uro i hagen. "Hun er død, hun er borte. Vi har balsamert kroppen hennes. Hun ligger på divanen, og hun er død, stakkars lille." 
Jesus gikk inn til all den støyen og sa: "Vær stille. Hold fred. Jenta er ikke død; hun sover bare." 
Og de lo av Ham. Vet du hva Han gjorde da? Da de lo av Guds Ord, sendte Han dem ut. Det var en god ting å gjøre, ikke sant? Han sendte dem ut.

text:
How could He do any miracles with such as that around?
Well, one time He had to take a man and lead him plumb out of the city so He could heal him on the account of so much unbelief. He came to His own country where He was raised up at, He couldn't do nothing: no works. They said, "We heard He'd done miracles over there. Let me see Him do them now."
And all the people that knew Him when He raised up as a boy: "This is a carpenter's son. We knowed His dad, and we knowed them all. We know His brothers and sisters. We know they're a bunch of poor people down there. Where'd He get this wisdom? What school did He come out of?"
Jesus marveled at their unbelief. He didn't stay there but a little bit, He traveled on. Then He said, "A prophet is not without honor, except among his own people, in his own county." That's right. And it's been that way ever since. Now, 'cause that's the Word of God, see. It has to be.
text_no:
Hvordan kunne Han utføre noen mirakler under slike omstendigheter?
En gang måtte Han lede en mann helt ut av byen for å kunne helbrede ham på grunn av så mye vantro. Da Han kom til Sitt eget hjemsted, hvor Han hadde vokst opp, kunne Han ikke gjøre noe. De sa, "Vi har hørt at Han gjorde mirakler der borte. La oss se Ham gjøre det nå."
Og alle som kjente Ham fra da Han var gutt: "Dette er tømmermannens sønn. Vi kjente Hans far, vi kjente dem alle. Vi kjenner Hans brødre og søstre. Vi vet at de er en gjeng fattige folk der nede. Hvor fikk Han denne visdommen fra? Hvilken skole kom Han fra?"
Jesus undret seg over deres vantro. Han oppholdt Seg der bare en kort stund, før Han reiste videre. Da sa Han, "En profet er ikke uten ære, bortsett fra blant sitt eget folk, i sin egen hjemby." Det stemmer, og slik har det alltid vært. Fordi det er Guds Ord, ser du. Det må være slik.

text:
But they laughed at Him. And what He said was the Word of God. And so then He said them words, and He put them all out of the house. And look who He took with Him: Peter, James, and John---love, faith, and charity … I mean, faith, and hope, and charity. Peter, James, and John---those three. That's what you want to take along with you. Hope, faith, and charity walked into the sick room.
And notice, He'd taken the father and mother; put all the rest of them out. Now, He's alone with the broken-hearted, with little Jairus and his wife who believed Him with all their heart. He said, "I told you not to fear; you shall see the glory of God." All right.
Then as He walks on down into the house, looks on the couch. There laid the poor little girl, laying there, dead, laying out on the couch, about twelve-years-old. And Jesus walked over to where she was, took a hold of her hand, and spoke in an unknown tongue: "Tabitha," called out.
Out there,
text_no:
Men de lo av Ham. Det Han sa, var Guds Ord. Deretter sa Han disse ordene og ba dem alle forlate huset. Se hvem Han tok med seg: Peter, Jakob og Johannes—kjærlighet, tro og veldedighet … jeg mener, tro, håp og kjærlighet. Peter, Jakob og Johannes—disse tre. Det er det du vil ha med deg. Håp, tro og kjærlighet gikk inn i sykestuen.
Merk at Han tok med seg faren og moren og ba alle de andre gå ut. Nå er Han alene med de sorgfulle, med lille Jairus og hans kone som trodde på Ham av hele sitt hjerte. Han sa: "Jeg ba dere om ikke å frykte; dere skal se Guds herlighet." Greit.
Så da Han gikk inn i huset, så Han på sofaen. Der lå den stakkars lille jenta, død, omtrent tolv år gammel. Jesus gikk bort til henne, tok hennes hånd og talte i ukjente tunger: "Talitha," ropte Han.

text:
they was all around. Look on the outside, all doubting, said, "Well, the very idea. That man putting us out of that house. Oh, wait till we get a hold of that Jairus. Mmm, just wait till we get him. Oh, my. The doctor said the child was going to die, and it done just what the doctor said. The child is dead. And here he is now bringing disgrace on our church. Mmm, just wait till he comes out of that house. We'll church him right now. Wait till the general council meets. We'll sure show him what to do. We'll fix him up. Just wait till they have the next conference. We sure won't have that guy around here no more."
Jesus, with all that bank of unbelief outside, He was empowered with a Spirit that could speak out into another land; out into another language. And He called the spirit of the dead from out of the land yonder and said, "Return back!" And the girl that was laying dead, by the touch of His hand, rose again.
Said, "Now give her something to eat, she's weak. She's going to get all right." Said, "Don't tell nobody about this now. It don't have to be put in all kinds of papers, and advertised all around." All right.
He was the man of the hour, He was; but He knew it Himself. God knew it, and that was sufficient. All right.
text_no:
De var overalt. På utsiden sto de tvilende og sa: "For en idé. Den mannen kaster oss ut av huset. Vent til vi får tak i den Jairus. Vent bare til vi får ham. Å, min.
